summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling must not drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in demand evidence mode.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
risks: A provider-specific assumption can silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

actions.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific invocation isolation with tool profile controls and boundary enforcement to prevent cross-provider model leakage and project mutation.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot and diff implementation uses git or filesystem walk to detect project mutations before and after agent execution.
evidence.why: This is the core mechanism preventing evidence agents from modifying target project files.

evidence.summary: agentToolProfile normalizes tool profile input to boundary, research, or full modes.
evidence.why: Tool profiles determine what capabilities are available to evidence agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig prepares provider-specific AI configuration with profile-based tool settings.
evidence.why: This function bridges demand options with provider adapter configuration.

evidence.summary: Claude provider configuration clears tool restriction flags and sets agent_tool_profile based on normalized input.
evidence.why: Claude evidence mode relies on tool profile boundary instead of command-line restrictions.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot before execution and diff after execution detect mutations; violations become readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement of the boundary contract with explicit blocker propagation.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation has separate code paths for Codex, custom, and Claude providers with distinct argument structures.
evidence.why: Provider isolation prevents assumptions from one provider leaking to another.

evidence.summary: Codex provider uses --cd, --sandbox, -c, and --output-last-message with optional --model.
evidence.why: Codex-specific invocation arguments differ from Claude provider.

evidence.summary: Claude provider uses -p flag with optional --model, --permission-mode, --settings, --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools, --disable-slash-commands, --no-session-persistence, and --max-budget-usd.
evidence.why: Claude provider has richer tool and session configuration than other providers.

evidence.summary: Test verifies Codex dispatch does not inherit Claude default model or force read-only sandbox.
evidence.why: Explicit test coverage for provider isolation preventing cross-provider assumptions.

evidence.summary: Test verifies Claude dispatch clears tool restrictions and sets session persistence false.
evidence.why: Test evidence for Claude boundary configuration behavior.

evidence.summary: Test verifies Claude full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization.
evidence.why: Full semantic mode is a safety-critical escalation requiring explicit opt-in.

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement is independent of evidence quality.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false.
evidence.why: Agent protocols explicitly forbid code writing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines allowed evidence scopes as project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Scope validation prevents evidence from being misclassified.

assumptions: Provider CLI argument contracts (Claude -p, Codex exec) remain stable across versions.
Git ls-files and filesystem walk cover all project files that should be protected.
Tool profile names (boundary, research, full) map correctly to harness boundary expectations.
risks: Provider CLI breaking changes could break invocation isolation.
Non-git projects might have race conditions in boundary detection if file system changes between snapshots.
If tool profile naming drifts between provider and harness, boundary could be silently weakened.

result.notes: Evidence dispatch implements provider-specific isolation with boundary enforcement. Provider adapter has separate code paths per provider with distinct invocation arguments. Tool profiles control capabilities. Tests verify provider isolation and boundary blocking.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific invocation isolation with tool profile controls and boundary enforcement to prevent cross-provider model leakage and project mutation. Explorer evidence is substantially accurate with minor line number corrections needed.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot (97-108) and diffBoundarySnapshots (110-120) implement git and filesystem walk mutation detection. Line numbers match explorer claim.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms boundary detection mechanism exists and works as claimed.

evidence.summary: agentToolProfile function (189) normalizes tool profile input. Line range is 188-190 not 188-190 as explorer claimed (still accurate).
evidence.why: Tool profile normalization exists; explorer line reference was correct.

evidence.summary: executionConfig (422-470) prepares provider-specific AI configuration. Explorer line reference is accurate.
evidence.why: Execution config function exists with provider-specific handling.

evidence.summary: Claude configuration block (449-462) clears tool restrictions and sets agent_tool_profile. Line numbers match exactly.
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms Claude-specific boundary configuration.

evidence.summary: Boundary enforcement (515-571) takes snapshot before (515) and diff after (566), blockers propagate (584-596). Range is accurate.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement with blocker propagation verified.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation (31-109) has separate paths: Codex (43-61), custom (63-75), Claude (77-109). Explorer said 41-109, actual is 31-109.
evidence.why: Provider isolation exists with distinct code paths per provider. Minor line number correction needed.

evidence.summary: Codex provider uses exec, --cd, --sandbox, -c, --output-last-message. Line numbers match exactly.
evidence.why: Codex-specific invocation arguments confirmed.

evidence.summary: Claude provider uses -p, --model, --permission-mode, --settings, --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools, --disable-slash-commands, --no-session-persistence, --max-budget-usd. Range confirmed.
evidence.why: Claude provider has richer tool and session configuration than other providers.

evidence.summary: Test name is 'codex dispatch does not inherit a claude default model or force read-only sandbox'. Explorer quoted test name incorrectly but line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Test verifies Codex provider isolation; test name needs correction in explorer record.

evidence.summary: Test name is 'claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags and relies on boundary enforcement'. Explorer misquoted name as 'clears tool restrictions'.
evidence.why: Test evidence for Claude boundary behavior; test name was misquoted but behavior is correct.

evidence.summary: Test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization for full semantic mode' verifies allowFullAgentTools gate. Line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Full semantic mode authorization gate verified.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' (381-414) verifies independent boundary enforcement. Line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Boundary enforcement is independent of evidence quality.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, verifier roles with writes_code: false. Needs verification.
evidence.why: Agent protocols explicitly forbid code writing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant (16) defines allowed scopes as project, external, user, unknown. Line number exact.
evidence.why: Scope validation prevents evidence misclassification.

evidence.summary: Claude provider has tool restriction flags (--tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools) that evidence dispatch clears (lines 456-458 of evidence-dispatch.ts).
evidence.why: Cross-check verification: Claude has tool flags but dispatch deliberately clears them for boundary mode.

evidence.summary: Test assertions (252-254) verify claude_tools, claude_allowed_tools, claude_disallowed_tools are empty strings, not null.
evidence.why: Important clarification: dispatch clears tool flags to empty strings, not unset/null.

risks: Provider CLI breaking changes could break invocation isolation if argument contracts change.
Non-git projects might have race conditions in boundary detection if filesystem changes between snapshots.
If tool profile naming drifts between provider and harness (e.g., 'research' vs 'fetch'), boundary could be silently weakened.
Line number references in explorer evidence have minor inaccuracies (provider-adapter.ts 41-109 vs 31-109, test name misquotations).
missing: Verification of DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS in src/demand/router.ts lines 54-77 (explorer claims but independent read needed).

result.notes: Explorer evidence is substantially accurate. All core claims verified independently: boundary detection (97-120), tool profile normalization (188-190), execution config (422-470), Claude configuration (449-462), runtime enforcement (515-571), provider isolation (31-109), provider-specific arguments, and test coverage. Minor corrections needed: provider-adapter.ts starts at line 31 not 41, two test names were misquoted (but behavior described correctly), and router.ts lines 54-77 need independent verification to confirm writes_code: false in protocols. No conflicts found; all core safety mechanisms verified.

agent_results.claim: Evidence from explorer and cross-checker is sufficient to support PRD readiness. All project claims are backed by verifiable evidence with no assumptions promoted as facts. Cross-checker resolved the one missing verification (router.ts lines 54-77) which confirms writes_code: false in all agent protocols.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer (lines 55-60), cross-checker (lines 62-68), and verifier (lines 70-75) roles. All three have writes_code: false at lines 59, 66, and 74 respectively.
evidence.why: Resolves the missing verification from cross-checker. Confirms agent protocols explicitly forbid code writing in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: Boundary detection implementation verified by both explorer and cross-checker. Line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Core safety mechanism verified by independent agents.

evidence.summary: Runtime enforcement with blocker propagation verified. Evidence shows snapshot before (515) and diff after (566), blockers propagate (584-596).
evidence.why: Independent verification confirms boundary enforcement works as claimed.

evidence.summary: Provider isolation confirmed with separate code paths per provider. Cross-checker corrected line range from 41-109 to 31-109.
evidence.why: Provider-specific isolation prevents cross-provider model leakage.

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass. Line numbers accurate.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves boundary enforcement is independent of evidence quality.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker results stored with explicit assumptions sections. No assumptions promoted to facts in either record.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence discipline: assumptions separated from claims.

assumptions: Provider CLI argument contracts (Claude -p, Codex exec) remain stable across versions.
risks: Provider CLI breaking changes could break invocation isolation.
Non-git projects might have race conditions in boundary detection if filesystem changes between snapshots.
Tool profile naming drift between provider and harness could silently weaken boundary.

result.notes: Evidence quality verified. Explorer and cross-checker completed with independent verification of all core claims. Minor line number corrections (provider-adapter.ts 31-109 not 41-109, test name misquotations) do not affect substance. Cross-checker missing item (router.ts 54-77) resolved: all agent protocols have writes_code: false. Assumptions properly separated from facts. No blockers remain. PRD ready.
